today_summary: GCM26 settled $4,039.0 (Jul 1 scrape) — up $0.3 from Tuesday's $4,038.7 close and net +$29.2 over the past week, but essentially round-tripping after peaking near $4,096 mid-week. The chain remains call-skewed (P/C OI 0.59; 130,919 calls vs 76,627 puts) with max pain glued at $4,800, 18.8% above spot and too distant at 28 DTE to act as a near-term pivot. ATM IV sits at 24.17%, implying a 1σ daily range of ±$61.50 ($3,977-$4,101). Net GEX across the ±20% window is -$139.3 — still deeply negative, with dealers short gamma and no stabilizing flip point until $3,235, roughly 20% below spot.
five_day_thesis: Over the last 5 sessions spot round-tripped: rallying from $4,009.8 to a $4,096 high on 06-29 before fading back to $4,039, netting just +$29.2 (+0.7%) — while ATM IV expanded from 22.42% to a 26.43% peak on 06-26 and now sits +1.75pp higher at 24.17%. That combination — flat net price, rising IV — signals the market is pricing in more event risk without confirming direction. Net GEX has stayed negative every single day (-241 → -193 → -129 → -177 → -139), deepest on 06-25 as spot pushed toward its high, and the gamma flip strike has held in a tight $3,225-$3,280 band roughly 20% below spot, meaning there is no nearby stabilizing level and dealer hedging keeps amplifying moves in both directions rather than dampening them. Structural walls are frozen: the $4,000 put wall and $5,370 call wall haven't moved a single strike in 5 sessions, and max pain has sat glued to $4,800 the entire week — positioning is stale, not chasing price.
what_changed: Spot round-tripped: $4,009.8 → $4,096 high (06-29) → $4,039 today, net +$29.2 (+0.7%) over 5 sessions
ATM IV expanded 22.42% → 24.17% (+1.75pp), peaking at 26.43% on 06-26 — vol pricing in more risk without matching realized price movement
Net GEX stayed negative all 5 sessions (-241.3 → -139.3) — persistent dealer short-gamma regime, no flip to positive
Gamma flip strike held in a narrow $3,225-$3,280 band, ~20% below spot — no nearby stabilizer
Put wall ($4,000) and call wall ($5,370) unchanged all 5 days — stale positioning, no fresh OI chasing price
Max pain frozen at $4,800 (+18.8% above spot) for the full week — too distant at 28 DTE to exert near-term pull
prediction: Bias is range-bound-to-cautiously-bullish inside the 1σ band $3,977-$4,101, with the $4,000 put wall as the nearest floor and the $4,300/$4,500 call OI clusters as the next magnets on a breakout. Because net GEX is deeply negative and the flip strike sits ~20% below spot, a push through $4,100 or a break below $4,000 is likely to accelerate rather than mean-revert — this is a volatility-amplifying tape, not a pinning one. Invalidation: a daily close below $3,975 opens a retest of the $3,900/$3,850 heavy put strikes.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Spot pinned near $4,039 with 5-day IV expansion (+1.75pp) unconfirmed by price — negative GEX means breakouts accelerate, not fade.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Buy the dip into the $4,000 put wall on volume confirmation; it is the nearest OI floor in range.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Sustained trade above → path to $4,150 then the $4,300 call OI cluster. Sustained trade below → retest of $4,000 put wall, then $3,900.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: -1.1% from entry, below the 1σ daily low ($3,977.5) and the $4,000 put wall.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 near-term call OI cluster (2,000 ct), take 50% off. T2 major call wall (3,341 ct), exit remaining.
day_trade_cta.risk_note: Net GEX -139.3 across the ±20% window, gamma flip 20% below spot — expect 1.5%+ daily ranges; reduce size 30-50%.
